families.name: Hydrolytic bond cleavage
family_boundary_guardrails: Do not propagate labels across glycosidase, thiol protease, phosphorolysis, or nonhydrolytic metal-transfer chemistry without mechanism-text support.
Treat metal ligation as insufficient for hydrolase propagation unless water activation or hydrolytic bond cleavage is explicit.

families.name: Pyridoxal-phosphate chemistry
family_boundary_guardrails: Do not propagate between PLP decarboxylation, transamination, racemization, and beta-elimination subclasses without active-site lysine and substrate-class agreement.

families.name: Radical rearrangement chemistry
family_boundary_guardrails: Do not propagate radical labels between SAM/Fe-S and adenosylcobalamin systems solely by radical wording.
Require cofactor-family support or explicit mechanism text before treating radical families as compatible.

families.name: Flavin redox chemistry
family_boundary_guardrails: Separate oxygen insertion from hydride-transfer redox unless FAD/FMN context and reaction-center text support the subclass.
NAD-only hydride transfer is not enough to propagate a flavin label.

families.name: Heme peroxide and oxidase chemistry
family_boundary_guardrails: Do not propagate from generic heme or electron-transfer proteins into peroxide activation without distal activation residue or peroxide/oxygen chemistry support.

propagation_guardrails.rule: Family propagation requires shared mechanism family plus conserved catalytic role signatures; sequence cluster membership alone cannot promote labels above bronze.
Domain architecture can raise review priority but cannot promote across ontology families without reaction-center agreement.
Family annotations must be treated as weak evidence when mechanism text or cofactor evidence conflicts with the seed fingerprint.

When UniRef/CATH/InterPro are unavailable, use entry name, M-CSA mechanism text, cofactor ligands, and pocket geometry as auditable local proxies; keep propagated labels bronze until direct evidence promotes them.
